executive_summary: CISA has added two critical vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog—CVE-2026-33017 (Langflow RCE) and CVE-2026-33634 (Aquasecurity Trivy supply chain flaw)—both actively exploited within hours of disclosure. TeamPCP threat actors continue their supply chain campaign, compromising the Telnyx PyPI package (742,000 downloads) to deliver malware via WAV files. Iran-linked Handala hackers claim to have breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email account, publishing excerpts online. Canada-based GeoMechanics Technologies was listed as a victim by the Akira ransomware group. BreachForums v5 itself was breached by ShinyHunters, exposing credentials of 340,000 users.

items.description: Langflow remote code execution vulnerability exploited within hours of disclosure

canada_landscape.summary: GeoMechanics Technologies, a Canadian company, was publicly listed as a victim by the Akira ransomware group on March 27, 2026. No CCCS (Canadian Centre for Cyber Security) advisories were identified in this 48-hour period. Multiple articles referenced Canada in general threat landscape contexts but did not report specific breaches of Canadian critical infrastructure or major enterprises.

report_2w.executive_summary: Oracle issued an emergency patch for a critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-21992) in Oracle Identity Manager that poses an immediate threat to organizations. A sophisticated supply chain attack compromised the LiteLLM PyPI package (versions 1.82.7-1.82.8), stealing SSH keys, cloud credentials, and Kubernetes secrets while installing persistent backdoors—organizations using this AI library should rotate all credentials immediately. Iranian threat group Handala is actively targeting dissidents and journalists through messaging platforms with malware, while separately a self-propagating wiper malware called CanisterWorm is targeting Iranian infrastructure. The FBI and CISA warn that Russian hackers are hijacking Signal and WhatsApp accounts through phishing campaigns. An iPhone exploit chain called DarkSword, capable of compromising millions of devices, has leaked online.

canada_landscape.summary: No direct Canadian cybersecurity incidents or CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. However, Canadian intelligence experts are warning about potential Iranian sleeper cell threats to Canadians, and a cyber-research group indicates Canadians will face a 'tsunami' of transnational repression in coming years. No breaches affecting major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported.
